skill.name: Schema Markup & Structured Data
skill.description: Design, validate, and optimize schema.org structured data for eligibility, correctness, and measurable SEO impact. Uses a Schema Eligibility & Impact Index to determine whether markup is appropriate and likely to produce benefits.
skill.summary: AI-powered schema markup assistant that helps create valid JSON-LD structured data for Google rich results while preventing invalid or spammy markup.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Risk score: 0. Static analysis found no suspicious patterns. The skill is a pure prompt template that guides AI to help with schema markup creation and validation. Contains appropriate safety measures including a Schema Eligibility & Impact Index to prevent invalid markup.

content.value_statement: Creating proper schema.org markup is complex and errors can hurt SEO. This skill provides a structured approach to design valid JSON-LD that qualifies for Google rich results while avoiding common mistakes.
actual_capabilities: Calculate Schema Eligibility & Impact Index to determine if markup is appropriate
Design valid JSON-LD structured data for common schema types (Article, Product, FAQ, Event)
Validate schema against Google rich result eligibility requirements
Identify and prevent invalid, misleading, or spammy markup patterns
Provide implementation guidance for static sites, React/Next.js, and CMS platforms
Audit existing schema for errors and optimization opportunities
limitations: Does not guarantee rich results will appear in Google search
Cannot add content that does not exist on the page
Does not access live Google documentation or APIs
Does not validate schema in real-time against Google's testing tools

use_cases.title: New Schema Implementation
use_cases.description: Add schema markup to a new landing page, blog post, or product page with proper eligibility assessment
use_cases.target_user: Web developers and content creators adding structured data

use_cases.title: Schema Audit and Fixes
use_cases.description: Review existing schema for errors, warnings, or policy violations and provide corrective recommendations

use_cases.title: Multi-Entity Page Strategy
use_cases.description: Design schema for pages with multiple related entities using @graph for proper entity relationships
use_cases.target_user: Technical SEO experts and web architects

prompt_templates.title: Basic Schema Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create JSON-LD schema markup for a [page_type] page about [topic]. The page contains [visible_content]. What schema type should I use and what is the eligibility score?
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple schema generation for a single page

prompt_templates.title: Fix Existing Schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this JSON-LD schema [paste schema] for errors or policy violations. Identify what needs to be fixed and provide corrected markup.
prompt_templates.scenario: Fixing validation errors in existing schema

prompt_templates.title: Rich Result Eligibility Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate whether a [schema_type] schema qualifies for Google rich results. What are the eligibility requirements and does this content meet them?
prompt_templates.scenario: Checking if schema qualifies for rich results

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Entity Schema Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design schema markup for a page with [entities]. How should I use @graph to represent the relationships between [primary_entity] and [secondary_entities]?
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating schema for complex pages with multiple entities

output_examples.input: Create FAQ schema for a support page with 5 questions and answers about our product returns policy
output_examples.output:
Eligibility Index: 88/100 (Strong Candidate)

Schema Strategy: FAQPage is appropriate since questions and answers are visible. Score reflects high content-schema alignment, Google-supported type, complete required properties, valid JSON-LD, and low spam risk.

JSON-LD Implementation: [valid FAQPage schema with question/answer pairs]

Validation Checklist: Valid JSON-LD, passes Rich Results Test, matches visible content, meets Google eligibility rules

output_examples.input: Add product schema to an e-commerce product page
output_examples.output:
Eligibility Assessment Required. Before implementing, I need to know: What content is visible on the page? Which rich result are you targeting? Is this content templated or editorial?

Note: Product schema requires price, availability, and offers to be visible on the page. Schema must match visible content exactly.

best_practices: Always calculate the Schema Eligibility & Impact Index before implementing to ensure markup is justified
Use Google Rich Results Test to validate schema before deployment
Match schema exactly to visible content - never add markup for content not shown on the page
anti_patterns: Adding schema for content that is not visible to users
Using unsupported schema types that have no Google eligibility
Creating misleading or exaggerated claims in schema to game rich results

faq.question: Does schema markup guarantee rich results in Google?
faq.answer: No. Schema markup helps Google understand your content but does not guarantee rich results. The skill explicitly states it does not guarantee rich results.

faq.question: What is the Schema Eligibility & Impact Index?
faq.answer: A diagnostic score from 0-100 that evaluates whether schema markup is appropriate for a page. Scores below 55 mean you should not implement schema.

faq.question: Can I add schema for content not visible on my page?
faq.answer: No. Schema must reflect visible, user-facing content. Adding hidden schema is against Google guidelines and can result in penalties.

faq.question: Which schema types are eligible for Google rich results?
faq.answer: Google supports specific types including Article, Product, FAQ, HowTo, Event, Recipe, and more. The skill focuses on Google-supported types for maximum SEO value.

faq.question: How do I validate my schema markup?
faq.answer: Use the Google Rich Results Test, Schema.org Validator, and monitor Search Console enhancements. The skill requires validation before deployment.

faq.question: Can this skill help with multi-entity pages?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers using @graph for pages with multiple related entities while following the rule of one primary entity per page.
